scenario: You get a call from someone saying they mistakenly transferred ₹5000 to your PhonePe account. They send a screenshot showing the transfer and ask you to send it back. You check your bank balance and there is no new money. What do you do?
options: Trust the screenshot and send ₹5000 back to be a good person.
Send ₹5000 back but only after they promise it's not a scam.
Refuse to send anything since your actual bank balance hasn't changed.
feedback: Correct! Scammers easily fake screenshots. Always trust your actual bank statement, never a screenshot sent on WhatsApp.

scenario: You receive an automatic IVR call claiming to be from FedEx. It says a package with your Aadhaar has been intercepted by Customs with illegal goods. Press 1 for customer care.
options: Press 1 to clear up the misunderstanding.
Wait on the line to see what happens next.
feedback: Correct! This is the start of the 'Digital Arrest' scam. FedEx and Customs do not make automated calls like this. Disconnect immediately.

scenario: Your bank's official app sends a push notification inside the app (not an SMS) asking you to review your annual KYC status by visiting your nearest branch.
options: Ignore it, it's probably a scam.
Click the notification to read the details inside the secure app, or visit the branch.
Call the number in a random SMS you received earlier to ask about it.
feedback: Correct! In-app notifications from the official banking app asking you to visit a branch are legitimate and safe.

scenario: You list an old sofa on OLX. A buyer immediately agrees to the price without negotiating and says they will send money via a QR code. They send you a QR code on WhatsApp and ask you to scan it to 'receive' the payment.
options: Scan the QR code to receive the money quickly.
Ask them to send the QR code via email instead for security.
Block the buyer. You scan QR codes to PAY, never to RECEIVE.
feedback: Correct! This is a classic OLX scam. Scanning a QR code ALWAYS deducts money from your account. You NEVER scan a QR code to receive money.
